questions.questionText: The acceleration due to gravity on the surface of the Earth is g. At a height equal to the radius of the Earth (R), the acceleration due to gravity becomes:
At what depth below the surface of the Earth does the acceleration due to gravity become half of its value on the surface? (R = Radius of Earth)
The time period of a satellite orbiting close to the surface of the Earth is T. If it is moved to an orbit of radius 4 times the Earth's radius, its new time period will be:
The escape velocity from the surface of Earth is 11.2 km/s. If a planet has twice the mass and twice the radius of Earth, the escape velocity from its surface will be:
A body of mass m is projected vertically upwards from the surface of Earth with a speed equal to half of the escape velocity. The maximum height reached by it is: (R = Radius of Earth)
